summary: Problem 6 (Bilinear Forms on Weighted Trees and Lattice Embeddings) remains in early stages with an estimated ~10% completeness per the latest verification assessment. The proof attempt is largely incomplete, with the verifier corroborating the critic's finding that core arguments are missing or unsubstantiated. Substantial mathematical work is required before a complete proof can be assembled.
problems: Proof completeness is only ~10%; the bulk of the argument connecting bilinear forms on weighted trees to lattice embeddings has not been written.
The verifier confirmed the critic's review that genuine gaps exist — key steps appear to be asserted rather than rigorously established.
No verified bridge from the weighted-tree bilinear form structure to the target lattice embedding criterion is present, leaving the central claim unsupported.
The issue is still 'in_progress' with no clear roadmap of intermediate lemmas needed to close the gaps.
highlights: The critic's review was independently assessed as accurate by the verifier, indicating reliable internal quality control.
The problem has been clearly scoped, and the verification process has identified specifically where the argument breaks down, giving a concrete starting point.

suggested_todos.title: Draft the missing core lemma linking weighted-tree bilinear forms to lattice embeddability conditions
Address each specific gap flagged in the verifier and critic assessments with rigorous justifications
Construct an explicit proof outline enumerating required intermediate lemmas and their dependencies
Add concrete examples/small cases (e.g., low-rank trees) to validate the proposed embedding construction
Re-run verification after each lemma is completed to track incremental completeness gains
